translation.text: They both ran to the door and, in the struggle, she tore his shirt at the back. They found her husband at the door. She cried, "Shall not the man who wished to violate your wife be thrown into prison or sternly punished?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So they both raced each other to the door, and she tore his shirt from the back: they both found her lord near the door. She said: "What is the (fitting) punishment for one who formed an evil design against thy wife, but prison or a grievous chastisement?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Joseph) said: "It was she who wanted to seduce me." And a witness from her family testified: "If the shirt is torn from the front then the woman is speaking the truth, and he is a liar.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Yusuf, “It was she who lured me, that I may not guard myself” – and a witness from her own household testified; “If his shirt is torn from the front, then the woman is truthful and he has spoken incorrectly.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said he, 'It was she that solicited me'; and a witness of her folk bore witness, 'If his shirt has been torn from before then she has spoken truly, and he is one of the liars;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: it is she who solicited me against myself And a witness from her own household bare witness: if his shirt be rent in front, then she speaketh the truth and he is of the liars.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He [Yusuf (Joseph)] said: "It was she that sought to seduce me," - and a witness of her household bore witness (saying): "If it be that his shirt is torn from the front, then her tale is true and he is a liar!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “It was she who tried to seduce me.” A witness from her household suggested: “If his shirt is torn from the front: then she has told the truth, and he is the liar.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph said: "It is she who was trying to tempt me to herself." And a witness belonging to her own household testified (on grounds of circumstantial evidence): "If his shirt is torn from the front, then she is telling the truth and he is a liar.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He [Yusuf] said: "It was she that sought to seduce me;" and a witness of her household bore witness (saying): "If it be that his shirt is torn from the front, then her tale is true and he is a liar!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Joseph) said: She it was who asked of me an evil act. And a witness of her own folk testified: If his shirt is torn from before, then she speaketh truth and he is of the liars.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘It was she who solicited me.’ A witness of her own household testified [thus]: ‘If his shirt is torn from the front, she tells the truth and he lies.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He (Joseph) said: 'It was she who sought to seduce me' and a witness (an infant) of her people bore witness, saying: 'If his shirt is torn from the front, she is speaking the truth and he is one of the liars,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Joseph] said, "It was she who sought to seduce me." And a witness from her family testified. "If his shirt is torn from the front, then she has told the truth, and he is of the liars.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph said, "It was she who tried to seduce me." Someone from the household in confirmation of Joseph's statement said, "If his shirt it torn from the front, she has spoken the truth and he is lying,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: She sought to make me yield (to her); and a witness of her own family bore witness: If his shirt is rent from front, she speaks the truth and he is one of the liars:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Joseph said, "It was she who sought to seduce me." One of her household testified, "If his shirt is torn at the front then she is speaking the truth, and he is lying.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "It was she that sought to seduce me - from my (true) self." And one of her household saw (this) and bore witness, (thus):- "If it be that his shirt is rent from the front, then is her tale true, and he is a liar!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But if the shirt is torn from behind then she is a liar, and he speaks the truth."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And if his shirt is torn from behind, then the woman is a liar and he is truthful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: but if it be that his shirt has been torn from behind, then she has lied, and he is one of the truthful.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And if his shirt be rent from behind, then she lieth, and he is of the truth-tellers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "But if it be that his shirt is torn from the back, then she has told a lie and he is speaking the truth!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But if his shirt is torn from the back: then she has lied, and he is the truthful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: But if his shirt is torn from behind, then she has lied, and he is truthful."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "But if it be that his shirt is torn from the back, then she has told a lie and he is speaking the truth!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And if his shirt is torn from behind, then she hath lied and he is of the truthful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: but, if his shirt is torn from behind, she lied, and he is of the truthful'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But if his shirt is torn from the back, then she has lied, and he is of the truthful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: but if his shirt is torn from behind, she is lying and he is speaking the truth."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And if his shirt is rent from behind, she tells a lie and he is one of the truthful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But if it is torn from behind then she is lying, and he speaks the truth."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But if it be that his shirt is torn from the back, then is she the liar, and he is telling the truth!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When the husband saw the shirt torn at the back, he said: "Surely this is a woman's ruse, and the wiles of women are great.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When he saw his shirt was torn from behind he said, 'This is of your women's guile; surely your guile is great.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So when he saw his shirt rent from behind, he said: verily it is of the guile of ye women; verily the guile of ye women is mighty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So when he (her husband) saw his [(Yusuf's (Joseph)] shirt torn at the back; (her husband) said: "Surely, it is a plot of you women! Certainly mighty is your plot!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And when he saw that his shirt was torn from the back, he said, “This is a woman's scheme. Your scheming is serious indeed.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So when the husband saw Joseph's shirt torn from behind he exclaimed: "Surely, this is one of the tricks of you women; your tricks are indeed great.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So when he (her husband) saw his [Yusuf`s] shirt torn at the back, he (her husband) said: "Surely, it is a plot of you women! Certainly mighty is your plot!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So when he saw his shirt torn from behind, he said: Lo! this is of the guile of you women. Lo! the guile of you is very great.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: So when he saw that his shirt was torn from behind, he said, ‘This is [a case] of you women’s guile! Your guile is indeed great!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when he saw his shirt torn at the back, he said: 'This is one of your (women's) guiles. Your guile, (O woman), is great indeed!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when her husband saw his shirt torn from the back, he said, "Indeed, it is of the women's plan. Indeed, your plan is great.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When the master saw that Joseph's shirt was torn from behind, he told his wife, "This is some of your womanly guile in which you are certainly skillful.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when he saw his shirt rent from behind, he said: Surely it is a guile of you women; surely your guile is great:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So when he saw his shirt torn from behind [her husband] said, "This is the guile of you women. Your guile is great indeed.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So when he saw his shirt,- that it was torn at the back,- (her husband) said: "Behold! It is a snare of you women! truly, mighty is your snare!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Ignore this affair, O Joseph; and you, O woman, ask forgiveness for your sin, for you were surely errant."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O Yusuf! Disregard this – and O woman! Seek forgiveness for your sin; indeed you are of the mistaken.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Joseph, turn away from this; and thou, woman, ask forgiveness of thy crime; surely thou art one of the sinners.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Yusuf! turn away therefrom; and, thou woman! ask forgiveness for thy sin; verily thou hast been of the guilty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O Yusuf (Joseph)! Turn away from this! (O woman!) Ask forgiveness for your sin. Verily, you were of the sinful."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Joseph, turn away from this. And you, woman, ask forgiveness for your sin; you are indeed in the wrong.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph, disregard this. And you - woman - ask forgiveness for your sin, for indeed it is you who has been at fault."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O Yusuf! Turn away from this! (O woman!) Ask forgiveness for your sin, verily, you were of the sinful."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: O Joseph! Turn away from this, and thou, (O woman), ask forgiveness for thy sin. Lo! thou art of the faulty.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Joseph, let this matter alone, and you, woman, plead for forgiveness for your sin, for you have indeed been erring.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Joseph, turn away from this. And you, ask forgiveness for your sin. You are indeed one of the sinners'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Joseph, ignore this. And, [my wife], ask forgiveness for your sin. Indeed, you were of the sinful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph, stay away from such affairs and you, woman, ask forgiveness for your sin; the guilt is yours."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O Yusuf! turn aside from this; and (O my wife)! ask forgiveness for your fault, surely you are one of the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Joseph, overlook this: but you [wife], ask forgiveness for your sin, for you have done wrong."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O Joseph, pass this over! (O wife), ask forgiveness for thy sin, for truly thou hast been at fault!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: In the city the women gossiped: "The minister's wife longs after her page. He has captured her heart. We think she is in clear error."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And some women of the city said, “The governor’s wife is seducing her young slave; indeed his love has taken root in her heart; and we find her clearly lost.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Certain women that were in the city said, 'The Governor's wife has been soliciting her page; he smote her heart with love; we see her in manifest error.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And women in the city said; the wife of the AZiZ hath solicited her page against himself; he hath inflamed her with love; verily we behold her in error manifest.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And women in the city said: "The wife of Al-'Aziz is seeking to seduce her (slave) young man, indeed she loves him violently; verily we see her in plain error."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Some ladies in the city said, “The governor's wife is trying to seduce her servant. She is deeply in love with him. We see she has gone astray.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And some ladies in the city began to say: "The chief's wife, violently in love with her houseboy, is out to tempt him. We think she is clearly mistaken."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And women in the city said: "The wife of the `Aziz is seeking to seduce her (slave) young man, indeed she loves him violently; verily, we see her in plain error."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And women in the city said: The ruler's wife is asking of her slave-boy an ill-deed. Indeed he has smitten her to the heart with love. We behold her in plain aberration.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Certain women in the city said: 'The Governor's wife has sought to seduce her servant. He has made her heart stricken with love for him. Clearly, we perceive her to be in error'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And women in the city said, "The wife of al-'Azeez is seeking to seduce her slave boy; he has impassioned her with love. Indeed, we see her [to be] in clear error."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Some of the women in the town started to gossip saying, "The King's wife has tried to seduce her servant and has fallen madly in love with him. We think that she is in manifest error".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And women in the city said: The chiefs wife seeks her slave to yield himself (to her), surely he has affected her deeply with (his) love; most surely we see her in manifest error.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Women in the town began to gossip, "The nobleman's wife is trying to seduce her slave! Love for him consumes her heart! Indeed! We see her in manifest error."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Ladies said in the City: "The wife of the (great) 'Aziz is seeking to seduce her slave from his (true) self: Truly hath he inspired her with violent love: we see she is evidently going astray."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When she heard their slanderings, she sent for them and prepared a banquet, and gave each of them a knife (for paring fruit), and called (to Joseph): "Come out before them." When they saw him, the women were so wonderstruck they cut their hands, and exclaimed: "O Lord preserve us! He is no mortal but an honourable angel."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And when she heard of their secret talk, she sent for them and prepared cushioned mattresses for them and gave a knife to each one of them and said to Yusuf, “Come out before them!” When the women saw him they praised him and cut their hands, and said, “Purity is to Allah – this is no human being; this is not but an honourable angel!”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When she heard their sly whispers, she sent to them, and made ready for them a repast, then she gave to each one of them a knife. 'Come forth, attend to them,' she said. And when they saw him, they so admired him that they cut their hands, saying, 'God save us! This is no mortal; he is no other but a noble angel.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then, when she heard of their cunning talk, she sent unto them a messenger, and got ready for them a cushioned Couch, and gave a knife to each of them. And she said: come forth to them. Then when they saw him, they were astonished at him, and they made a cut in their hands, and said: how perfect is God! no man is he: he is naught but an angel noble.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So when she heard of their accusation, she sent for them and prepared a banquet for them; she gave each one of them a knife (to cut the foodstuff with), and she said [(to Yusuf (Joseph)]: "Come out before them." Then, when they saw him, they exalted him (at his beauty) and (in their astonishment) cut their hands. They said: "How perfect is Allah (or Allah forbid)! No man is this! This is none other than a noble angel!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And when she heard of their gossip, she invited them, and prepared for them a banquet, and she gave each one of them a knife. She said, “Come out before them.” And when they saw him, they marveled at him, and cut their hands. They said, “Good God, this is not a human, this must be a precious angel.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Hearing of their sly talk the chief's wife sent for those ladies, and arranged for them a banquet, and got ready couches, and gave each guest a knife. Then, while they were cutting and eating the fruit, she signalled Joseph: "Come out to them." When the ladies saw him they were so struck with admiration that they cut their hands, exclaiming: "Allah preserve us. This is no mortal human. This is nothing but a noble angel!"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So when she heard of their accusation, she sent for them and prepared a banquet for them; she gave each one of them a knife, and she said [to Yusuf]: "Come out before them." Then, when they saw him, they exalted him (at his beauty) and (in their astonishment) cut their hands. They said: "How perfect is Allah! No man is this! This is none other than a noble angel!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when she heard of their sly talk, she sent to them and prepared for them a cushioned couch (to lie on at the feast) and gave to every one of them a knife and said (to Joseph): Come out unto them! And when they saw him they exalted him and cut their hands, exclaiming: Allah Blameless! This is no a human being. This is not other than some gracious angel.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: When she heard of their machinations, she sent for them and arranged a repast, and gave each of them a knife, and said [to Joseph], ‘Come out before them.’ So when they saw him, they marvelled at him and cut their hands [absent-mindedly], and they said, ‘Good heavens! This is not a human being! This is but a noble angel!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: When she heard of their sly whispers, she sent for them and prepared a banquet. To each she gave a knife, (then called Joseph saying:) 'Come and attend to them' When they saw him, they were so taken with him that they cut their hands, and said: 'Allah save us! This is no mortal, he is no other but a noble angel'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when she heard of their scheming, she sent for them and prepared for them a banquet and gave each one of them a knife and said [to Joseph], "Come out before them." And when they saw him, they greatly admired him and cut their hands and said, "Perfect is Allah! This is not a man; this is none but a noble angel."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When she heard their gossiping, she invited them to her house for a banquet and gave a knife to each of them. Then she told Joseph to appear before them. When they saw Joseph, they were so amazed that they cut their hands and said, "Goodness gracious! He is not a mortal but is a charming angel!"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when she heard of their sly talk she sent for them and prepared for them a repast, and gave each of them a knife, and said (to Yusuf): Come forth to them. So when they saw him, they deemed him great, and cut their hands (in amazement), and said: Remote is Allah (from imperfection); this is not a mortal; this is but a noble angel.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When she heard of their intrigues, she sent for them and prepared a party for them. She gave a knife to each of them [to cut fruits, etc.] and then asked Joseph to appear before them. When the women saw him, they were greatly amazed [at his beauty], and they cut their hands, exclaiming, "God preserve us! This is no human being but a noble angel!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: When she heard of their malicious talk, she sent for them and prepared a banquet for them: she gave each of them a knife: and she said (to Joseph), "Come out before them." When they saw him, they did extol him, and (in their amazement) cut their hands: they said, "Allah preserve us! no mortal is this! this is none other than a noble angel!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: She said: "This is the one you blamed me for. I did desire his person, but he preserved himself from sin. Yet in case he does not do my bidding he will be put into prison and disgraced."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: She said, “This is he on whose account you used to insult me; and indeed I tried to entice him, so he safeguarded himself; and indeed if he does not do what I tell him to, he will surely be imprisoned, and will surely be humiliated.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'So now you see, she said. 'This is he you blamed me for. Yes, I solicited him, but he abstained. Yet if he will not do what I command him, he shall be imprisoned, and be one of the humbled.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: She said: that then is he in regard to whom ye reproached me. Assuredly solicited him against himself but he abstained; and if he doth not that which I command him, he shall surely be imprisoned and he shall surely be of the degraded.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: She said: "This is he (the young man) about whom you did blame me (for his love), and I did seek to seduce him, but he refused. And now if he refuses to obey my order, he shall certainly be cast into prison, and will be one of those who are disgraced."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: She said, “Here he is, the one you blamed me for. I did try to seduce him, but he resisted. But if he does not do what I tell him to do, he will be imprisoned, and will be one of the despised.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: She said: "So now you see! This is the one regarding whom you reproached me. Indeed I tried to tempt him to myself but he held back, although if he were not to follow my order, he would certainly be imprisoned and humiliated."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: She said: "This is he (the young man) about whom you did blame me, and I did seek to seduce him, but he refused. And now if he refuses to obey my order, he shall certainly be cast into prison, and will be one of those who are disgraced."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: She said: This is he on whose account ye blamed me. I asked of him an evil act, but he proved continent, but if he do not my behest he verily shall be imprisoned, and verily shall be of those brought low.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Whereupon she said: 'Now you see, this is he on whose account you blamed me. Yes, I sought to seduce him, but he was unyielding. If he declines to do what I command, he shall be imprisoned and be one of the humiliated'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She said, "That is the one about whom you blamed me. And I certainly sought to seduce him, but he firmly refused; and if he will not do what I order him, he will surely be imprisoned and will be of those debased."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: She said, "This is the one on whose account you subjected me to all this blame. I tried to seduce him but he abstained. If he does not yield to me, I shall order him to be locked up in prison to make him humble."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: She said: This is he with respect to whom you blamed me, and certainly I sought his yielding himself (to me), but he abstained, and if he does not do what I bid him, he shall certainly be imprisoned, and he shall certainly be of those who are in a state of ignominy.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: She said, "This is he about whom you have been blaming me! And, indeed, I did try to make him yield to me, but he was unyielding. Now, however, if he does not do as I bid him, he shall certainly be put in prison and be humiliated."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She said: "There before you is the man about whom ye did blame me! I did seek to seduce him from his (true) self but he did firmly save himself guiltless!.... and now, if he doth not my bidding, he shall certainly be cast into prison, and (what is more) be of the company of the vilest!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Joseph) prayed: "O Lord, dearer is prison than what they invite me to. Unless You turn their guiles away from me I shall succumb to their charms and thus become a pagan."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'My Lord, prison is dearer to me than that they call me to; yet if Thou turnest not from me their guile, then I shall yearn towards them, and so become one of the ignorant.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: my Lord: prison is dearer to me than that to which these women call me; and if Thou avertest not their guile from me should incline toward them and become of the Ignorant.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said:"O my Lord! Prison is more to my liking than that to which they invite me. Unless You turn away their plot from me, I will feel inclined towards them and be one (of those who commit sin and deserve blame or those who do deeds) of the ignorants."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “My Lord, prison is more desirable to me than what they call me to. Unless You turn their scheming away from me, I may yield to them, and become one of the ignorant.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph said: "My Lord! I prefer imprisonment to what they ask me to do. And if You do not avert from me the guile of these women, I will succumb to their attraction and lapse into ignorance."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! Prison is dearer to me than that to which they invite me. Unless You turn away their plot from me, I will feel inclined towards them and be one of the ignorant."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O my Lord! Prison is more dear than that unto which they urge me, and if Thou fend not off their wiles from me I shall incline unto them and become of the foolish.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! The prison is dearer to me than to what they invite me. If You do not turn away their schemes from me, I will incline towards them and become one of the senseless.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He supplicated: 'Lord, prison is dearer to me than that to which they invite me. Yet if You do not shield me from their guile I shall yield to them and be one of the ignorant'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, prison is more to my liking than that to which they invite me. And if You do not avert from me their plan, I might incline toward them and [thus] be of the ignorant."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph said, "Lord, prison is dearer to me than that which women want me to do. Unless You protect me from their guile, I shall be attracted to them in my ignorance".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: My Lord! the prison house is dearer to me than that to which they invite me; and if Thou turn not away their device from me, I will yearn towards them and become (one) of the ignorant.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Joseph said, "O my Lord! I would prefer prison to what these women are inviting me to do. And if You do not avert their guile from me, I may yield to them and so become one of the ignorant."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! the prison is more to my liking than that to which they invite me: Unless Thou turn away their snare from me, I should (in my youthful folly) feel inclined towards them and join the ranks of the ignorant."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: His Lord heard his prayer, and averted the women's wiles from him. He verily hears and knows everything.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So his Lord heard his prayer and repelled the women’s deceit away from him; indeed He only is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So his Lord answered him, and He turned away from him their guile; surely He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then his Lord answered to him, and averted their guile from him; verily He! He is the Hearer, the Knower!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So his Lord answered his invocation and turned away from him their plot. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Thereupon his Lord answered him, and diverted their scheming away from him. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereupon his Lord granted his prayer, and averted their guile from him. Surely He alone is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So his Lord answered his invocation and turned away from him their plot. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So his Lord heard his prayer and fended off their wiles from him. Lo! He is Hearer, Knower.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: His Lord answered him and He turned their guile away from him. Indeed, He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So his Lord responded to him and averted from him their plan. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: His Lord heard his prayers and protected him from their guile; He is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Thereupon his Lord accepted his prayer and turned away their guile from him; surely He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So his Lord answered him and warded off their guile. He is All Hearing and All Knowing.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So his Lord hearkened to him (in his prayer), and turned away from him their snare: Verily He heareth and knoweth (all things).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And yet in spite of having seen these clear proofs they found it proper to incarcerate him for a time.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then it seemed good to them, after they had seen the signs, that they should imprison him for a while.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Thereafter it occurred to them even after they had, the signs, to imprison him for a season.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then it appeared to them, after they had seen the proofs (of his innocence) to imprison him for a time.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then it occurred to them, after they had seen the signs, to imprison him for a while.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then it occurred to them to cast Joseph into prison for a while even though they had seen clear signs (of Joseph's innocence and of the evil ways of their ladies).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then it occurred to them, after they had seen the proofs (of his innocence), to imprison him for a time.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And it seemed good to them (the men-folk) after they had seen the signs (of his innocence) to imprison him for a time.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then it appeared to them, after they had seen all the signs [of his innocence], that they should confine him for some time.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then it seemed good to them, even after they had seen the signs, that they should imprison him for some time.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then it appeared to them after they had seen the signs that al-'Azeez should surely imprison him for a time.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Even after Joseph had been found innocent of any crime, the King and his people decided to imprison him for an appointed time (so that people would forget the incident).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then it occurred to them after they had seen the signs that they should imprison him till a time.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Yet, even after all the evidence they had seen, they thought it right to jail him for a time.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then it occurred to the men, after they had seen the signs, (that it was best) to imprison him for a time.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Two other youths were imprisoned along with him. Said one of them: "I dreamt that I was pressing grapes;" and the other: "I dreamt that I was carrying bread on my head, and the birds were pecking at it. You tell us the meaning of this. You seem to be a righteous man."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And there entered the prison with him two youths. Said one of them, 'I dreamed that I was pressing grapes. Said the other, 'I dreamed that I was carrying on my head bread, that birds were eating of. Tell us its interpretation; we see that thou art of the good-doers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And there entered with him into the prison two pages. One of them said: verlly I saw myself pressing wine; and the other said: verily I saw myself carrying upon my head bread whereof the birds were eating. declare unto us the interpretation thereof, verily we see thee of the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And there entered with him two young men in the prison. One of them said: "Verily, I saw myself (in a dream) pressing wine." The other said: "Verily, I saw myself (in a dream) carrying bread on my head and birds were eating thereof." (They said): "Inform us of the interpretation of this. Verily, we think you are one of the Muhsinun (doers of good - see V. 2:112)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Two youth entered the prison with him. One of them said, “I see myself pressing wine.” The other said, “I see myself carrying bread on my head, from which the birds are eating. Tell us their interpretation—we see that you are one of the righteous.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And with Joseph two other slaves entered the prison. One of them said: "I saw myself pressing wine in a dream"; and the other said: "I saw myself carrying bread on my head of which the birds were eating." Both said: "Tell us what is its interpretation; for we consider you to be one of those who do good."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And there entered with him two young men in the prison. One of them said: "Verily, I saw myself (in a dream) pressing wine." The other said: "Verily, I saw myself (in a dream) carrying bread on my head and birds were eating thereof." (They said): "Inform us of the interpretation of this. Verily, we think you are one of the doers of good."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And two young men went to prison with him. One of them said: I dreamed that I was pressing wine. The other said: I dreamed that I was carrying upon my head bread whereof the birds were eating. Announce unto us the interpretation, for we see thee of those good (at interpretation).

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: There entered the prison two youths along with him. One of them said, ‘I dreamt that I am pressing grapes.’ The other said, ‘I dreamt that I am carrying bread on my head from which the birds are eating.’ ‘Inform us of its interpretation,’ [they said], ‘for indeed we see you to be a virtuous man.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Two young men went to prison with him. One of them said: 'I saw (in a vision) that I was pressing grapes' And the other said: 'I saw (in a vision) that I was carrying bread upon my head, and that birds ate of it. Tell us its interpretation, for we can see you are among the good'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And there entered the prison with him two young men. One of them said, "Indeed, I have seen myself [in a dream] pressing wine." The other said, "Indeed, I have seen myself carrying upon my head [some] bread, from which the birds were eating. Inform us of its interpretation; indeed, we see you to be of those who do good."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Two young men were also sent to serve prison sentences (for different reasons). One of them said, "I had a dream in which I was brewing wine." The other one said, "In my dream I was carrying some bread on my head and birds were eating that bread." They asked Joseph if he would interpret their dreams. They said, "We believe you to be a righteous person."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And two youths entered the prison with him. One of them said: I saw myself pressing wine. And the other said: I saw myself carrying bread on my head, of which birds ate. Inform us of its interpretation; surely we see you to be of the doers of good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Two young men entered the prison along with him. One of them said, "I saw myself [in a dream] I was pressing wine." The other said, "I dreamed I was carrying bread on my head from which the birds were eating. Tell us their meaning; for we see that you are one who does good [to all]."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Now with him there came into the prison two young men. Said one of them: "I see myself (in a dream) pressing wine." said the other: "I see myself (in a dream) carrying bread on my head, and birds are eating, thereof." "Tell us" (they said) "The truth and meaning thereof: for we see thou art one that doth good (to all)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Joseph) answered: "I will give you its interpretation before the food you are served arrives. This knowledge is one of the things my Lord has taught me. I have given up the religion of those who do not believe in God and deny the life to come.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Yusuf, “The food which you get will not reach you, but I will tell you the interpretation of this before it comes to you; this is one of the sciences my Lord has taught me; indeed I did not accept the religion of the people who do not believe in Allah and are deniers of the Hereafter.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'No food shall come to you for your sustenance, but ere it comes to you I shall tell you its interpretation. That I shall tell you is of what God has taught me. I have forsaken the creed of a people who believe not in God and who moreover are unbelievers in the world to come.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: there will not come to you any food wherewith ye are provided but I shall have declared unto the interpretation thereof ere it cometh unto you. that is of that which my Lord hath taught me; verily I have abandoned the creed of a people who believe not in Allah and who in the Hereafter are disbelievers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "No food will come to you (in wakefulness or in dream) as your provision, but I will inform (in wakefulness) its interpretation before it (the food) comes. This is of that which my Lord has taught me. Verily, I have abandoned the religion of a people that believe not in Allah and are disbelievers in the Hereafter (i.e. the Kan'aniun of Egypt who were polytheists and used to worship sun and other false deities).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “No food is served to you, but I have informed you about it before you have received it. That is some of what my Lord has taught me. I have forsaken the tradition of people who do not believe in God; and regarding the Hereafter, they are deniers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph said: "I will inform you about the interpretation of the dreams before the arrival of the food that is sent to you. This knowledge is part of what I have been taught by my Lord. I have renounced the way of those who do not believe in Allah, and who deny the Hereafter,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "No food will come to you as your provision, but I will inform its interpretation before it comes. This is of that which my Lord has taught me. Verily, I have abandoned the religion of a people that believe not in Allah and are disbelievers in the Hereafter."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: The food which ye are given (daily) shall not come unto you but I shall tell you the interpretation ere it cometh unto you. This is of that which my Lord hath taught me. Lo! I have forsaken the religion of folk who believe not in Allah and are disbelievers in the Hereafter.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘Before the meals you are served come to you I will inform you of its interpretation. That is among things my Lord has taught me. Indeed, I renounce the creed of the people who have no faith in Allah and who [also] disbelieve in the Hereafter.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'Before any food comes to feed either of you, I will give you its interpretation. That which I will tell you has been taught to me by Allah. I reject the belief of a nation who do not believe in Allah and disbelieve in the Everlasting Life.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "You will not receive food that is provided to you except that I will inform you of its interpretation before it comes to you. That is from what my Lord has taught me. Indeed, I have left the religion of a people who do not believe in Allah, and they, in the Hereafter, are disbelievers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Joseph) said, "To prove that my interpretation of your dream is true, I can tell you what kind of food you will receive even before it comes to you. My Lord has given me such talents. I have given up the tradition of the people who do not believe in God and the Day of Judgment

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: There shall not come to you the food with which you are fed, but I will inform you both of its interpretation before it comes to you; this is of what my Lord has taught me; surely I have forsaken the religion of a people who do not believe in Allah, and they are deniers of the hereafter:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Joseph said to them, "I shall inform you of the interpretation of your dreams before your meal is brought to you. This is a part of the knowledge that my Lord has taught me. I have renounced the religion of the people who do not believe in God and who deny the Hereafter.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Before any food comes (in due course) to feed either of you, I will surely reveal to you the truth and meaning of this ere it befall you: that is part of the (duty) which my Lord hath taught me. I have (I assure you) abandoned the ways of a people that believe not in Allah and that (even) deny the Hereafter.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I follow the faith of my fathers, of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. We cannot associate anyone with God. This is among God's favours to us and to all mankind; but most men are not grateful.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And I have chosen the religion of my forefathers, Ibrahim and Ishaq and Yaqub; it is not rightful for us to ascribe anything as a partner to Allah; this is a grace of Allah upon us and upon mankind, but most men are not thankful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And I have followed the creed of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. Not ours is it to associate aught with God. That is of God's bounty to us, and to men; but most men are not thankful.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And I have followed the creed of my fathers, Ibrahim and Is'haq and ya'qub: it is not for us to associate aught with Allah. That is of Allah's and upon mankind; but grace upon us most of mankind thank not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And I have followed the religion of my fathers, - Ibrahim (Abraham), Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob)], and never could we attribute any partners whatsoever to Allah. This is from the Grace of Allah to us and to mankind, but most men thank not (i.e. they neither believe in Allah, nor worship Him).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “And I have followed the faith of my forefathers, Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob. It is not for us to associate anything with God. This is by virtue of God’s grace upon us and upon the people, but most people do not give thanks.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and I have adopted the way of my forefathers - Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. It is not for us to associate any with Allah in His Divinity. It is out of Allah's grace upon us and upon mankind (that He did not require of us to serve any other than Allah), and yet most people do not give thanks.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And I have followed the religion of my fathers, - Ibrahim, Ishaq and Ya'qub and never could we attribute any partners whatsoever to Allah. This is from the grace of Allah to us and to mankind, but most men thank not.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And I have followed the religion of my fathers, Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. It never was for us to attribute aught as partner to Allah. This is of the bounty of Allah unto us (the seed of Abraham) and unto mankind; but most men give not thanks.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: I follow the creed of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. It is not for us to associate anything with Allah. Such is the favor of Allah to us, and to mankind. Yet most people do not give thanks.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And I have followed the religion of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. And it was not for us to associate anything with Allah. That is from the favor of Allah upon us and upon the people, but most of the people are not grateful.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and I have embraced the religion of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. We are not supposed to consider anything equal to God. This is part of God's blessing to us and the people, but most people do not give thanks.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And I follow the religion of my fathers, Ibrahim and Ishaq and Yaqoub; it beseems us not that we should associate aught with Allah; this is by Allah's grace upon us and on mankind, but most people do not give thanks:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I follow the religion of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob; and it is not for us to associate anyone with God as a partner. This is of God's grace upon us and upon mankind; even though most men are not grateful.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And I follow the ways of my fathers,- Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; and never could we attribute any partners whatever to Allah: that (comes) of the grace of Allah to us and to mankind: yet most men are not grateful.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Tell me) O fellow-prisoners, are a number of gods better, or one God omnipotent?

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O both my fellow-prisoners! Are various lords better, or One Allah, the Dominant above all?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Say, which is better, my fellow-prisoners -- many gods at variance, or God the One, the Omnipotent?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O My two fellow-prisoners! are sundry lords better of Allah, the One, the Subduer

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O two companions of the prison! Are many different lords (gods) better or Allah, the One, the Irresistible?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O My fellow inmates, are diverse lords better, or God, the One, the Supreme?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Fellow-prisoners! Is it better that there be diverse lords, or just Allah, the One, the Irresistible?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O two companions of the prison! Are many different lords (gods) better or Allah, the One, the Irresistible"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: O my fellow-prisoners! Are divers lords better, or Allah the One, Almighty?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My fellowprisoners, say which is better, many gods at variance, or Allah the One, the Conqueror?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: O [my] two companions of prison, are separate lords better or Allah, the One, the Prevailing?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "My fellow-prisoners can many different masters be considered better than One All-dominant God?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O my two mates of the prison! are sundry lords better or Allah the One, the Supreme?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: O my two fellow-prisoners! Are many diverse lords better, or God, the One, the Almighty?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my two companions of the prison! (I ask you): are many lords differing among themselves better, or the One Allah, Supreme and Irresistible?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (What) you worship besides Him are nothing but names that you and your fathers have assigned, for which no sanction has been sent down by God. Authority belongs to God alone. He commands that you worship none but Him. This is the right way; but most men are ignorant.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “You do not worship anything besides Him, but which are merely names coined by you and your forefathers – Allah has not sent down any proof regarding them; there is no command but that of Allah; He has commanded that you do not worship anyone except Him; this is the proper religion, but most people do not know.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: That which you serve, apart from Him, is nothing but names yourselves have named, you and your fathers; God has sent down no authority touching them. Judgment belongs only to God; He has commanded that you shall not serve any but Him. That is the right religion; but most men know not.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Ye worship not, beside Him, but names ye have named, ye and your fathers: Allah hath not sent down for them any warranty. Judgment is but Allah's; He hath commanded that ye worship not except Him. That is the right religion, but most of the mankind know not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "You do not worship besides Him but only names which you have named (forged), you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. The command (or the judgement) is for none but Allah. He has commanded that you worship none but Him (i.e. His Monotheism), that is the (true) straight religion, but most men know not.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “You do not worship, besides Him, except names you have named, you and your ancestors, for which God has sent down no authority. Judgment belongs to none but God. He has commanded that you worship none but Him. This is the right religion, but most people do not know.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Those whom you serve beside Him are merely idle names that you and your fathers have fabricated, without Allah sending down any sanction for them. All authority to govern rests only with Allah. He has commanded that you serve none but Him. This is the Right Way of life, though most people are altogether unaware.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "You do not worship besides Him but only names which you have named (forged) - you and your fathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. The command is for none but Allah. He has commanded that you worship none but Him; that is the straight religion, but most men know not."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Those whom ye worship beside Him are but names which ye have named, ye and your fathers. Allah hath revealed no sanction for them. The decision rests with Allah only, Who hath commanded you that ye worship none save Him. This is the right religion, but most men know not.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: That which you worship, other than Him, are nothing but names which you and your fathers have named and for which Allah has sent down no authority. Judgement rests with Allah alone. He has commanded you to worship none except Him. That is the right religion, yet most people do not know.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: You worship not besides Him except [mere] names you have named them, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. Legislation is not but for Allah. He has commanded that you worship not except Him. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: What you worship instead of God are no more than empty names that you and your fathers have given to certain things. God has not given any authority to such names. Judgment belongs to no one but God. He has commanded you to worship nothing but Him. This is the only true religion, but most people do not know.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: You do not serve besides Him but names which you have named, you and your fathers; Allah has not sent down any authority for them; judgment is only Allah's; He has commanded that you shall not serve aught but Him; this is the right religion but most people do not know:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: All those you worship instead of Him are mere names you and your forefathers have invented, names for which God has sent down no authority: all power belongs to God alone, and He orders you to worship none but Him: this is the true faith, though most people do not realize it.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "If not Him, ye worship nothing but names which ye have named,- ye and your fathers,- for which Allah hath sent down no authority: the command is for none but Allah: He hath commanded that ye worship none but Him: that is the right religion, but most men understand not...

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O fellow-prisoners, one of you will serve wine to your master, the other will be crucified and the birds will peck at his brain. Determined is the matter of your inquiry."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O my two fellow-prisoners! One of you will give his lord (the king) wine to drink; regarding the other, he will be crucified, therefore birds will eat from his head; the command has been given concerning the matter you had enquired about.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Fellow-prisoners, as for one of you, he shall pour wine for his lord; as for the other, he shall be crucified, and birds will eat of his head. The matter is decided whereon you enquire.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O My two fellow-prisoners; as for one of you twain he will pour out wine for his lord; and as for the other, he will be crucified, and the birds will eat from off his head; O Thus is decreed the affair whereof ye twain enquired.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O two companions of the prison! As for one of you, he (as a servant) will pour out wine for his lord (king or master) to drink; and as for the other, he will be crucified and birds will eat from his head. Thus is the case judged concerning which you both did inquire."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O my fellow inmates! One of you will serve his master wine; while the other will be crucified, and the birds will eat from his head. Thus the matter you are inquiring about is settled.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Fellow-prisoners! (This is the interpretation of your dreams): one of you will serve wine to his lord (the king of Egypt). As for the other, he will be crucified and birds will eat of his head. The question concerning what you asked has thus been decided."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O two companions of the prison! As for one of you, he will pour out wine for his master to drink; and as for the other, he will be crucified and birds will eat from his head. Thus is the case judged concerning which you both did inquire."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: O my two fellow-prisoners! As for one of you, he will pour out wine for his lord to drink; and as for the other, he will be crucified so that the birds will eat from his head. Thus is the case judged concerning which ye did inquire.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My fellowprisoners, one of you will pour wine for his lord whereas the other will be crucified, and birds will peck at his head. The matter you inquired about has been decided.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: O two companions of prison, as for one of you, he will give drink to his master of wine; but as for the other, he will be crucified, and the birds will eat from his head. The matter has been decreed about which you both inquire."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "Fellow-prisoners, your dreams tell that one of you will serve wine to his master and the other will be crucified and his head consumed by the birds. Judgment has already been passed about the meaning of the dreams that you asked about."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O my two mates of the prison! as for one of you, he shall give his lord to drink wine; and as for the other, he shall be crucified, so that the birds shall eat from his head, the matter is decreed concerning which you inquired.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: O my two fellow-prisoners, one of you will serve wine to his lord, the other of you will be crucified and birds will feed off his head. The matter about which you have been seeking my opinion has been so decreed."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my two companions of the prison! As to one of you, he will pour out the wine for his lord to drink: as for the other, he will hang from the cross, and the birds will eat from off his head. (so) hath been decreed that matter whereof ye twain do enquire"...

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And (Joseph) asked the man he knew would be released: "Remember me to your lord;" but Satan made him forget to mention this to his lord, and Joseph remained in prison for a number of years.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And of the two, Yusuf said to the one whom he sensed would be released, “Remember me, in the company of your lord”; so Satan caused him to forget to mention Yusuf to his lord, he therefore stayed in prison for several years more.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then he said to the one he deemed should be saved of the two, 'Mention me in thy lord's presence.' But Satan caused him to forget to mention him to his master, so that he continued in the prison for certain years.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he said to one of them who he imagined would be saved: mention me in the presence of thy lord. Then the Satan caused him to forget to mention him to his lord, so that he tarried in the prison several years.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And he said to the one whom he knew to be saved: "Mention me to your lord (i.e. your king, so as to get me out of the prison)." But Shaitan (Satan) made him forget to mention it to his Lord [or Satan made [(Yusuf (Joseph)] to forget the remembrance of his Lord (Allah) as to ask for His Help, instead of others]. So [Yusuf (Joseph)] stayed in prison a few (more) years.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And he said to the one he thought would be released, “Mention me to your master.” But Satan caused him to forget mentioning him to his master, so he remained in prison for several years.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Joseph said to the one of the two prisoners who he knew would be set free: "Mention me in your lord's presence." But Satan caused him to forget mentioning this to his lord (the ruler of Egypt) and so Joseph languished in prison for several years.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And he said to the one whom he knew to be saved: "Mention me to your king." But Shaytan made him forget to mention it to his master. So [Yusuf] stayed in prison a few (more) years.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he said unto him of the twain who he knew would be released: Mention me in the presence of thy lord. But Satan caused him to forget to mention it to his lord, so he (Joseph) stayed in prison for some years.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then he said to the one whom he knew would be delivered from among the two: ‘Mention me to your master.’ But Satan caused him to forget mentioning [it] to his master. So he remained in the prison for several years.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And he said to the one of the two who he knew would be saved: 'Mention me in the presence of your lord' But satan made him forget to mention him to his master, so that he remained in prison for a certain number years.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he said to the one whom he knew would go free, "Mention me before your master." But Satan made him forget the mention [to] his master, and Joseph remained in prison several years.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph asked the one, whom he knew would not be executed, to mention his case to his Lord. Satan caused that man to forget all about Joseph and his case. Thus, Joseph remained in prison for some years.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he said to him whom he knew would be delivered of the two: Remember me with your lord; but the Shaitan caused him to forget mentioning (it) to his lord, so he remained in the prison a few years.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said to the one he thought would be saved, "Mention me to your master." However Satan made him forget to mention him to his master, and so Joseph remained in prison for a number of years.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And of the two, to that one whom he consider about to be saved, he said: "Mention me to thy lord." But Satan made him forget to mention him to his lord: and (Joseph) lingered in prison a few (more) years.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (One day) the king said (to his courtiers): "I saw seven fat cows in a dream being devoured by seven lean ones, and seven ears of corn that were green and seven others that were seared. O courtiers, tell me the significance of my dream, if you know how to interpret them."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And the king said, “I saw in a dream seven healthy cows whom seven lean cows were eating, and seven green ears of corn and seven others dry; O court-members! Explain my dream, if you can interpret dreams.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And the king said, 'I saw in a dream seven fat kine, and seven lean ones devouring them; likewise seven green ears of corn, and seven withered. My counsellors, pronounce to me upon my dream, if you are expounders of dreams.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the king said: verily I saw seven fat kine which seven lean ones are devouring and seven green cornears and seven others dry. O ye chiefs! give me an answer in regard to my vision if a vision ye are wont to expound.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And the king (of Egypt) said: "Verily, I saw (in a dream) seven fat cows, whom seven lean ones were devouring - and of seven green ears of corn, and (seven) others dry. O notables! Explain to me my dream, if it be that you can interpret dreams."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: The king said, “I see seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green spikes, and others dried up. O elders, explain to me my vision, if you are able to interpret visions.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And once the king said: "I have dreamt that there are seven fat cows and seven lean cows are devouring them, and there are seven fresh green ears of corn and seven others dry and withered. My nobles! Tell me what is the interpretation of this dream, if you are well-versed in interpretation of dreams."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And the king (of Egypt) said: "Verily, I saw (in a dream) seven fat cows, whom seven lean ones were devouring, and seven green ears of corn, and (seven) others dry. O notables! Explain to me my dream, if it be that you can interpret dreams."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the king said: Lo! I saw in a dream seven fat kine which seven lean were eating, and seven green ears of corn and other (seven) dry. O notables! Expound for me my vision, if ye can interpret dreams.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The king said: 'I saw in a vision seven fatted cows being devoured by seven lean ones; and seven green ears of corn and seven others withered. My counselors, tell me the meaning of my vision, if you can interpret visions'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [subsequently] the king said, "Indeed, I have seen [in a dream] seven fat cows being eaten by seven [that were] lean, and seven green spikes [of grain] and others [that were] dry. O eminent ones, explain to me my vision, if you should interpret visions."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Sometimes later), the King dreamt that seven lean cows were eating seven fat ones and that there were seven green ears of corn and seven dry ones. He asked the nobles to tell him the meaning of his dream if they were able to.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the king said: Surely I see seven fat kine which seven lean ones devoured; and seven green ears and (seven) others dry: O chiefs! explain to me my dream, if you can interpret the dream.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The king said, "I saw [in a dream] seven fat cows which seven lean ones were eating, also seven green ears of corn and seven others which were dry. Tell me the meaning of this vision, my nobles, if you can interpret visions."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The king (of Egypt) said: "I do see (in a vision) seven fat kine, whom seven lean ones devour, and seven green ears of corn, and seven (others) withered. O ye chiefs! Expound to me my vision if it be that ye can interpret visions."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "They were only confused dreams," they said; "we do not know how to interpret them."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They answered, “These are confused dreams – and we do not know the interpretation of dreams.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'A hotchpotch of nightmares!' they said. 'We know nothing of the interpretation of nightmares.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: medleys of dreams! and in the interpretation of dreams we are not skilled.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "Mixed up false dreams and we are not skilled in the interpretation of dreams."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “Jumbles of dreams, and we know nothing of the interpretation of dreams.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "These are confused dreams, and we do not know the interpretation of such dreams."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "Mixed up false dreams and we are not skilled in the interpretation of dreams."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They answered: Jumbled dreams! And we are not knowing in the interpretation of dreams.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'They are confused nightmares, nor do we know anything of the interpretation of visions'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "[It is but] a mixture of false dreams, and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They replied, "It is a confused dream and we do not know the meaning of such dreams."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: Confused dreams, and we do not know the interpretation of dreams.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "These are confusing dreams and we do not know the interpretation of such dreams."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "A confused medley of dreams: and we are not skilled in the interpretation of dreams."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Then the servant, who of the two had been released, remembering (Joseph), said: "I will give you its interpretation; let me go for it."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then said the one who had been delivered, remembering after a time, 'I will myself tell you its interpretation; so send me forth.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then the one of the twain, who was saved, and now recollected himself after a period, said: I, even I, shall declare unto you the interpretation thereof; so send me forth.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then the man who was released (one of the two who were in prison), now at length remembered and said: "I will tell you its interpretation, so send me forth."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: The one who was released said, having remembered after a time, “I will inform you of its interpretation, so send me out.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then of the two prisoners, the one who had been set free, now remembered, after the lapse of a long period, what Joseph had said. He said: "I will tell you the interpretation of this dream; just send me (to Joseph in prison)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then the man who was released, now at length remembered and said: "I will tell you its interpretation, so send me forth."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he of the two who was released, and (now) at length remembered, said: I am going to announce unto you the interpretation, therefore send me forth.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Said the one of the two who had been delivered, remembering [Joseph] after a long time: ‘I will inform you of its interpretation; so let me go [to meet Joseph in the prison].’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: After all that time, the one of the two who had been saved remembered, he said: 'I will interpret it for you, so let me go'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But the one who was freed and remembered after a time said, "I will inform you of its interpretation, so send me forth."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The man who was in prison with Joseph and who was released, recalled after so many years Joseph's (ability to interpret dreams) and said, "I can tell you the meaning of this dream if you allow me to go (to the prison and ask the man who knows the meanings of dreams)."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And of the two (prisoners) he who had found deliverance and remembered after a long time said: I will inform you of its interpretation, so let me go:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then one of the two men who had been released and who, after a long time, remembered, said, "I shall tell you its interpretation; therefore, give me leave to go [to Joseph in prison]."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But the man who had been released, one of the two (who had been in prison) and who now bethought him after (so long) a space of time, said: "I will tell you the truth of its interpretation: send ye me (therefore)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (And coming to the prison) he said: "O Joseph the truthful, tell us (the meaning of) seven fat cows being devoured by seven lean ones, and seven green ears of corn and seven seared, that I may go back to the people and tell them."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O Yusuf! O truthful one! Explain for us the seven healthy cows which seven lean cows were eating and the seven green ears of corn and seven others dry, so I may return to the people, possibly they may come to know.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'Joseph, thou true man, pronounce to us regarding seven fat kine, that seven lean ones were devouring, seven green ears of corn, and seven withered; haply I shall return to the men, haply they will know.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Yusuf, O saint! give an answer unto us in regard to seven fat kine which seven lean ones are devouring and seven green corn-ears and seven others dry; haply may return unto the people; haply they may learn.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: (He said): "O Yusuf (Joseph), the man of truth! Explain to us (the dream) of seven fat cows whom seven lean ones were devouring, and of seven green ears of corn, and (seven) others dry, that I may return to the people, and that they may know."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Joseph, O man of truth, inform us concerning seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green spikes, and others dried up, so that I may return to the people, so that they may know.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then he went to Joseph and said to him: "Joseph, O truthfulness incarnate, tell the true meaning of the dream in which seven fat cows are devoured by seven lean ones; and there are seven green ears of corn and seven others dry and withered so that I may return to the people and they may learn."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: (He said): "O Yusuf, the man of truth! Explain to us seven fat cows whom seven lean ones were devouring, and seven green ears of corn, and (seven) others dry, that I may return to the people, and that they may know."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (And when he came to Joseph in the prison, he exclaimed): Joseph! O thou truthful one! Expound for us the seven fat kine which seven lean were eating and the seven green ears of corn and other (seven) dry, that I may return unto the people, so that they may know.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: ‘Joseph,’ [he said], ‘O truthful one, give us your opinion concerning seven fat cows who are eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and [seven] others dry, that I may return to the people so that they may know [the truth of the matter].’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (He said:) 'Joseph, the truthful, tell us of the seven fatted cows that were devoured by seven lean ones also of the seven green ears of corn and the other seven which were withered, so that I can go back to the people and then they will then know'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [He said], "Joseph, O man of truth, explain to us about seven fat cows eaten by seven [that were] lean, and seven green spikes [of grain] and others [that were] dry - that I may return to the people; perhaps they will know [about you]."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (He went to the prison) and said to Joseph, "You are a man of truth. Would you tell me the meaning of a dream in which seven fat cows eat up seven lean ones and the meaning of seven green ears of corn and seven dry ones? I hope you can tell me the right meaning and save people from confusion."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Yusuf! O truthful one! explain to us seven fat kine which seven lean ones devoured, and seven green ears and (seven) others dry, that I may go back to the people so that they may know.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: "O truthful Joseph!" he said, "Tell us the meaning of a dream in which seven fat cows are being eaten by seven lean ones, and there are seven green ears of corn and seven others which are dry, so that I may return to my people and inform them."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O Joseph!" (he said) "O man of truth! Expound to us (the dream) of seven fat kine whom seven lean ones devour, and of seven green ears of corn and (seven) others withered: that I may return to the people, and that they may understand."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "Sow as usual for seven years, and after reaping leave the corn in the ears, except the little you need for food.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'You shall sow seven years after your wont; what you have harvested leave in the ear, excepting a little whereof you eat.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: ye shall sow seven years as is your went; and that which ye reap leave in its ears, except a little whereof ye may eat.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [(Yusuf (Joseph)] said: "For seven consecutive years, you shall sow as usual and that (the harvest) which you reap you shall leave in ears, (all) - except a little of it which you may eat.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “You will farm for seven consecutive years. But whatever you harvest, leave it in its spikes, except for the little that you eat.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph said: "You will cultivate consecutively for seven years. Leave in the ears all that you have harvested except the little out of which you may eat.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: [Yusuf] said: "For seven consecutive years, you shall sow as usual and that which you reap you shall leave it in the ears, (all) except a little of it which you may eat."

translation.author: Pickthall
