translation.text: Then, while Joseph was having their provisions loaded, he put his drinking-cup in his brother's saddlebag. And then a herald cried: "Travellers, you are thieves."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So when he furnished them forth with their provisions, he put the bowl in his brother's bag. Then a crier cried: "O you (in) the caravan! Surely, you are thieves!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when he provided them with their provision, he put the drinking-cup in his brother's saddlebag, and then a crier cried: O camel-riders! Lo! ye are surely thieves!

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: When he had furnished them with their provision, he put the drinking-cup into his brother’s saddlebag. Then a herald shouted: ‘O [men of the] caravan! You are indeed thieves!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when he had given them their provisions, he hid a drinkingcup in his brother's saddlebag. Then a herald called out after them: 'Cameleers, you are thieves!'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when he had furnished them with their supplies, he put the [gold measuring] bowl into the bag of his brother. Then an announcer called out, "O caravan, indeed you are thieves."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When he had furnished them with provisions, he placed the King's drinking cup in his own brother's baggage. Then someone shouted, "People of the caravan, you are thieves!"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when he furnished them with their provisions, (someone) placed the drinking cup in his brother's bag. Then a crier cried out: O caravan! you are most surely thieves.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And when he had given them their provisions, he placed a drinking-cup in his brother's pack. Then a crier called out after them, "Men of the caravan! You have committed theft!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: At length when he had furnished them forth with provisions (suitable) for them, he put the drinking cup into his brother's saddle-bag. Then shouted out a crier: "O ye (in) the caravan! behold! ye are thieves, without doubt!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They turned to them and asked: "What is it you have lost?"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They answered and turned towards them, “What is it you cannot find?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, turning to them, 'What is it that you are missing?'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said while they turned toward them: what is it that ye miss!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They, turning towards them, said: "What is it that you have missed?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, as they came towards them, “What are you missing?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Turning back they asked: "What have you lost?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They, turning towards them, said: "What is it that you have lost"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They cried, coming toward them: What is it ye have lost?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They turned back and asked: 'What have you lost'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said while approaching them, "What is it you are missing?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph's brothers turned around and asked, "What is missing?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said while they were facing them: What is it that you miss?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, turning towards him, "What is it that you have lost?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said, turning towards them: "What is it that ye miss?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "We cannot find the master's goblet. Whoever comes up with it will be given a camel-load of grain; I vouch for it."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'We are missing the king's goblet. Whoever brings it shall receive a camel's load; that I guarantee.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: we miss the king's cup; and for him who bringeth it shall be a camel-load; and thereof I am a guarantor.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "We have missed the (golden) bowl of the king and for him who produces it is (the reward of) a camel load; I will be bound by it."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “We are missing the king’s goblet. Whoever brings it will have a camel-load; and I personally guarantee it.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The officials said: "We have lost the king's cup." (And their chief added): "He who brings it shall have a camel-load of provisions, I guarantee that."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "We have lost the bowl of the king and for him who produces it is (the reward of) a camel load; and I will be bound by it."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: We have lost the king's cup, and he who bringeth it shall have a camel-load, and I (said Joseph) am answerable for it.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘We miss the king’s goblet.’ ‘Whoever brings it shall have a camel-load [of grain],’ [said the steward], ‘I will guarantee that.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'We are missing the goblet of the king' he replied. 'Whosoever restores it shall have a camelload, that I guarantee'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "We are missing the measure of the king. And for he who produces it is [the reward of] a camel's load, and I am responsible for it."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They were told, "The King's drinking cup is missing and whoever brings it will receive a camel's load of grain. I promise you that".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: We miss the king's drinking cup, and he who shall bring it shall have a camel-load and I am responsible for it.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: "We miss the royal measuring bowl," he replied. "He who brings it shall have a camel-load of corn. I pledge my word for it."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "We miss the great beaker of the king; for him who produces it, is (the reward of) a camel load; I will be bound by it."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "We swear by God. You know we did not come to commit any crime in the land, nor are we thieves."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “By Allah, you know very well that we did not come here to cause turmoil in the land, and nor are we thieves!”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'By God,' they said, 'you know well that we came not to work corruption in the land. We are not robbers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: by Allah! assuredly ye know that we came not to work corruption in the land, nor we have been thieves.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! Indeed you know that we came not to make mischief in the land, and we are no thieves!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “By God, you know we did not come to cause trouble in the land, and we are not thieves.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "By Allah, you certainly know that we did not come to act corruptly in this land, nor are we those who steal."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! Indeed you know that we came not to make mischief in the land, and we are no thieves!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: By Allah, well ye know we came not to do evil in the land, and are no thieves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'By Allah' they said, 'you know we did not come to do evil in this land. We are not thieves'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "By Allah, you have certainly known that we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we have not been thieves."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph's brothers said, "We swear by God, as you know, that we have not come to spread evil in the land and that we have not committed any theft."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: By Allah! you know for certain that we have not come to make mischief in the land, and we are not thieves.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "By God, you [ought to] know we have not come here to cause any trouble in the land. We are not thieves!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (The brothers) said: "By Allah! well ye know that we came not to make mischief in the land, and we are no thieves!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "What should be the punishment," they were asked, "in case you are liars?"

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'And what shall be its recompense if you are liars?'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: what shall be the meed of him, if ye are found liars!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They [Yusuf's (Joseph) men] said: "What then shall be the penalty of him, if you are (proved to be) liars."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “What shall be his punishment, if you are lying?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The officials said: "If you are lying, what will be the penalty for him who has stolen?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "What then shall be the penalty of him, if you are (proved to be) liars."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: And what shall be the penalty for it, if ye prove liars?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘What shall be its requital if you [prove to] be lying?’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'What shall be the recompense, if you prove to be lying'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: The accusers said, "Then what would be its recompense if you should be liars?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The Egyptians said, "What do you suggest should be the punishment for the thief, if it is proved that you are lying?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: But what shall be the requital of this, if you are liars?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The Egyptians asked them, "And if we find that you are lying, what penalty shall we mete out to you?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (The Egyptians) said: "What then shall be the penalty of this, if ye are (proved) to have lied?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "The punishment for that (should be)," they said, "that he in whose luggage it is found should be held as punishment. This is how we repay the wrong-doers."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'This shall be its recompense -- in whoever's saddlebag the goblet is found, he shall be its recompense. So we recompense the evildoers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: his meed shall be that he, in whose pack it is found, shall himself be recompense thereof. Thus we reccmpense the wrong- doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They [Yusuf's (Joseph) brothers] said: "The penalty should be that he, in whose bag it is found, should be held for the punishment (of the crime). Thus we punish the Zalimun (wrong-doers, etc.)!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “His punishment, if it is found in his bag: he will belong to you. Thus we penalize the guilty.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They replied: "He in whose saddlebag the cup is found, he himself shall be its recompense." Thus do we punish the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They [Yusuf's brothers] said: "His penalty should be that he, in whose bag it is found, should be held for the punishment (of the crime). Thus we punish the wrongdoers!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: The penalty for it! He in whose bag (the cup) is found, he is the penalty for it. Thus we requite wrong-doers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘The requital for it shall be that he in whose saddlebag it is found shall give himself over as its requital. Thus do we requite the wrongdoers.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They replied: 'Its recompense in whosoever's saddle bag it is found, he shall be its recompense. As such we punish the harmdoers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [The brothers] said, "Its recompense is that he in whose bag it is found - he [himself] will be its recompense. Thus do we recompense the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph's brothers replied, "In whosoever baggage it is found, that person will be your bondsman. Thus is the punishment of the unjust."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: The requital of this is that the person in whose bag it is found shall himself be (held for) the satisfaction thereof; thus do we punish the wrongdoers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They replied, "The penalty should be that he in whose saddlebag it is found, should be held [as bondman] to atone for the crime. That is how we punish the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "The penalty should be that he in whose saddle-bag it is found, should be held (as bondman) to atone for the (crime). Thus it is we punish the wrong-doers!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So he searched their saddle-bags before his brother's, then produced the cup from his brother's bag. That is how We planned an excuse for Joseph, for under the law of the king he could not detain his brother unless God so willed. We raise the status of whom We please. Over every man of knowledge there is one more knowing.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So he first searched their bags before his brother’s bag, then removed it from his brother’s bag; this was the plan We had taught Yusuf; he had no right to take his brother by the king’s law, except if Allah wills; We may raise in ranks whomever We will; and above every possessor of knowledge is another scholar.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So he made beginning with their sacks, before his brother's sack, then he pulled it out of his brother's sack. So We contrived for Joseph's sake; he could not have taken his brother, according to the king's doom, except that God willed. Whomsoever We will, We raise in rank; over every man of knowledge is One who knows.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then he began with their sacks before the sack of his brother; then he brought it forth from his brothers sack. In this wise We contrived for Yusuf. He was not one to take his brother by the law of the king, except that Allah willed. We exalt in degrees whomsoever We will, and above every knowing one is a Knower.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So he [Yusuf (Joseph)] began (the search) in their bags before the bag of his brother. Then he brought it out of his brother's bag. Thus did We plan for Yusuf (Joseph). He could not take his brother by the law of the king (as a slave), except that Allah willed it. (So Allah made the brothers to bind themselves with their way of "punishment, i.e. enslaving of a thief.") We raise to degrees whom We please, but over all those endowed with knowledge is the All-Knowing (Allah).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So he began with their bags, before his brother's bag. Then he pulled it out of his brother’s bag. Thus We devised a plan for Joseph; he could not have detained his brother under the king’s law, unless God so willed. We elevate by degrees whomever We will; and above every person of knowledge, there is one more learned.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then Joseph began searching their bags before searching his own brother's bag. Then he brought forth the drinking-cup from his brother's bag. Thus did We contrive to support Joseph. He had no right, according to the religion of the king (i.e. the law of Egypt), to take his brother, unless Allah so willed. We exalt whomsoever We will over others by several degrees. And above all those who know is the One Who truly knows.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So he [Yusuf] began (the search) in their bags before the bag of his brother. Then he brought it out of his brother's bag. Thus did We plan for Yusuf. He could not take his brother by the law of the king (as a slave), except that Allah willed it. We raise to degrees whom We will, but over all those endowed with knowledge is the All-Knowing (Allah).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then he (Joseph) began the search with their bags before his brother's bag, then he produced it from his brother's bag. Thus did We contrive for Joseph. He could not have taken his brother according to the king's law unless Allah willed. We raise by grades (of mercy) whom We will, and over every lord of knowledge there is one more knowing.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then he began with their sacks, before [opening] his brother’s sack. Then he took it out from his brother’s sack. Thus did We devise for Joseph’s sake. He could not have held his brother under the king’s law unless Allah willed [otherwise]. We raise in rank whomever We please, and above every man of knowledge is One who knows best.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He searched their bags before his brother's and then took out the goblet from his brother's bag. As such We directed Joseph. By the king's law he had no right to seize his brother unless Allah willed otherwise. We raise whom We will in rank; over every knowledgeable person is One who knows.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So he began [the search] with their bags before the bag of his brother; then he extracted it from the bag of his brother. Thus did We plan for Joseph. He could not have taken his brother within the religion of the king except that Allah willed. We raise in degrees whom We will, but over every possessor of knowledge is one [more] knowing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They searched their baggage before that of Joseph's real brother where at last they found it. Thus, We showed Joseph how to plan this; he would not have been able to take his brother under the King's law unless God had wanted it to be so. We give a high rank to whomever We want. Over every knowledgeable person is one more knowing.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So he began with their sacks before the sack of his brother, then he brought it out from his brother's sack. Thus did We plan for the sake of Yusuf; it was not (lawful) that he should take his brother under the king's law unless Allah pleased; We raise the degrees of whomsoever We please, and above every one possessed of knowledge is the All-knowing one.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He [the herald] searched their bags before his brother's and then took out the cup from his brother's bag. In this way, We devised a plan on behalf of Joseph. He could not have detained his brother under the King's law, unless God so willed. We exalt whoever We please: but above those who have knowledge there is One all knowing.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So he began (the search) with their baggage, before (he came to) the baggage of his brother: at length he brought it out of his brother's baggage. Thus did We plan for Joseph. He could not take his brother by the law of the king except that Allah willed it (so). We raise to degrees (of wisdom) whom We please: but over all endued with knowledge is one, the All-Knowing.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Said the brothers: "If he has stolen (no wonder), his brother had stolen before." But Joseph kept this secret and did not disclose it to them, and said (to himself): "You are worse in the degree of evil, for God knows better of what you allege."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “If he steals, then indeed his brother has stolen before”; so Yusuf kept this in his heart and did not reveal it to them; he replied within himself, “In fact, you are in a worse position; and Allah well knows the matters you fabricate.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'If he is a thief, a brother of his was a thief before.' But Joseph secreted it in his soul and disclosed it not to them, saying, 'You are in a worse case; God knows very well what you are describing.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: if he stealeth, then surely a brother of him hath stolen afore. But Yusuf concealed it in himself, and discovered it not unto them. He said: ye are in evil plight, and Allah is the Best Knower of that which ye ascribe.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They [(Yusuf's (Joseph) brothers] said: "If he steals, there was a brother of his [Yusuf (Joseph)] who did steal before (him)." But these things did Yusuf (Joseph) keep in himself, revealing not the secrets to them. He said (within himself): "You are in worst case, and Allah knows best the truth of what you assert!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “If he has stolen, a brother of his has stolen before.” But Joseph kept it to himself, and did not reveal it to them. He said, “You are in a worse situation, and God is Aware of what you allege.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "No wonder that he steals for a brother of his stole before." But Joseph kept his reaction to himself without disclosing the truth to them. He merely said to himself: "You are an evil lot. Allah knows well the truth of the accusation that you are making against me (to my face)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They [Yusuf's brothers] said: "If he steals, there was a brother of his [Yusuf] who did steal before (him). " But these things did Yusuf keep in himself, revealing not the secrets to them. He said (within himself): "You are in an evil situation, and Allah is the Best Knower of that which you describe!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: If he stealeth, a brother of his stole before. But Joseph kept it secret in his soul and revealed it not unto them. He said (within himself): Ye are in worse case, and Allah knoweth best (the truth of) that which ye allege.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘If he has stolen [there is no wonder]; a brother of his had stolen before.’ Thereupon Joseph kept the matter to himself and he did not disclose it to them. He said, ‘You are in a worse state! And Allah knows best what you allege.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'If he is a thief know then that a brother of his has committed theft before him' But Joseph kept it secret and did not reveal it to them. He said:'You are in a worse position. And Allah knows well what you describe'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "If he steals - a brother of his has stolen before." But Joseph kept it within himself and did not reveal it to them. He said, "You are worse in position, and Allah is most knowing of what you describe."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Joseph's) brothers said, "It's no wonder that he steals; a brother of his had stolen before him." Joseph noted their remarks, but did not utter a word. He said (to himself), "You are in a worse position. God knows best what you allege."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: If he steal, a brother of his did indeed steal before; but Yusuf kept it secret in his heart and did not disclose it to them. He said: You are in an evil condition and Allah knows best what you state.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "If he is a thief, a brother of his had [also] committed theft before him." But Joseph kept his secret and revealed nothing to them. He said [to himself], "Your deed was worse. God best knows the things you speak of."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "If he steals, there was a brother of his who did steal before (him)." But these things did Joseph keep locked in his heart, revealing not the secrets to them. He (simply) said (to himself): "Ye are the worse situated; and Allah knoweth best the truth of what ye assert!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "O Minister, he has an aged father, so keep one of us in his place. We see you are a virtuous man."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “O governor! He has a very aged father, so take one of us in his stead; indeed we witness your favours.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'Mighty prince, he has a father, aged and great with years; so take one of us in his place; we see that thou art one of the good-doers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: Aziz! verily he hath a father, an old man very aged; So take one of us in his stead; verily we behold thee to be of the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "O ruler of the land! Verily, he has an old father (who will grieve for him); so take one of us in his place. Indeed we think that you are one of the Muhsinun (good-doers - see V. 2:112)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “O noble prince, he has a father, a very old man, so take one of us in his place. We see that you are a good person.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "O powerful chief (al-aziz)! His father is an age-stricken man, (and in order that he may not suffer) seize one of us in his stead. We indeed consider you an excellent person."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "O `Aziz! Verily, he has an old father (who will grieve for him); so take one of us in his place. Indeed we think that you are one of the doers of good."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: O ruler of the land! Lo! he hath a very aged father, so take one of us instead of him. Lo! we behold thee of those who do kindness.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'Mighty prince, his father is old, advanced in years, take one of us instead of him. We can see you are amongst the gooddoers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "O 'Azeez, indeed he has a father [who is] an old man, so take one of us in place of him. Indeed, we see you as a doer of good."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "Noble Prince, his father is very old so please take one of us in his place. We believe that you are a righteous person".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: O chief! he has a father, a very old man, therefore retain one of us in his stead; surely we see you to be of the doers of good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "O exalted one, he has a very aged father, take one of us in his place. We can see that you are a very good man."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "O exalted one! Behold! he has a father, aged and venerable, (who will grieve for him); so take one of us in his place; for we see that thou art (gracious) in doing good."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "May God forgive us," he said, "if we hold any one but him with whom we found our property, or else we would be unjust."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'God forbid that we should take any other but him in whose possession we found the goods; for if we did so, we would be evildoers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: Allah forbid that we should take anyone but him with whom we found our stuff; verily then we should be the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "Allah forbid, that we should take anyone but him with whom we found our property. Indeed (if we did so), we should be Zalimun (wrong-doers)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “God forbid that we should arrest anyone except him in whose possession we found our property; for then we would be unjust.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Joseph said: "Allah forbid that we should seize any except him with whom we found our good. Were we to do so, we would surely be one of the wrong-doers."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "Allah forbid, that we should take anyone but him with whom we found our property. Indeed (if we did so), we should be wrongdoers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Allah forbid that we should seize save him with whom we found our property; then truly we should be wrong-doers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘God forbid that we should detain anyone except him with whom we found our wares, for then we would indeed be wrongdoers.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He replied: 'Allah forbid that we should seize any but he with whom our property was found, for then we should be harmdoers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "[I seek] the refuge of Allah [to prevent] that we take except him with whom we found our possession. Indeed, we would then be unjust."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He replied, "God forbid! How could I take someone in place of the thief? In doing so I would be committing injustice."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: Allah protect us that we should seize other than him with whom we found our property, for then most surely we would be unjust.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He replied, "God forbid that we should take anyone other than the person on whom we found our property. In that case, we would clearly be wrongdoers."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Allah forbid that we take other than him with whom we found our property: indeed (if we did so), we should be acting wrongfully.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When they despaired of (persuading) him, they went aside to confer. The oldest of them said: "You know that your father has pledged you in the name of God, and you have been guilty of iniquity in the case of Joseph before. I will not leave this place unless my father permits or God decides for me, for He is the best of all judges.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So when they did not anticipate anything from him, they went away and started consulting each other; their eldest brother said, “Do you not know that your father has taken from you an oath upon Allah, and before this, how you had failed in respect of Yusuf? Therefore I will not move from here until my father permits or Allah commands me; and His is the best command.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When they despaired of moving him, they conferred privily apart. Said the eldest of them, 'Do you not know how your father has taken a solemn pledge from you by God, and aforetime you failed regarding Joseph? Never will I quit this land, until my father gives me leave, or God judges in my favour; He is the best of judges.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when they despaired of him they counselled together privately. The eldest of them said: know ye not that your father hath taken an assurance from you before Allah? and aforetime ye have been remiss in your duty in respect of Yusuf; I so will by no means go forth from the land until my father giveth me leave or Allah judgeth for me, and He is the Best of judges.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So, when they despaired of him, they held a conference in private. The eldest among them said: "Know you not that your father did take an oath from you in Allah's Name, and before this you did fail in your duty with Yusuf (Joseph)? Therefore I will not leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah decides my case (by releasing Benjamin) and He is the Best of the judges.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And when they despaired of him, they conferred privately. Their eldest said, “Don’t you know that your father received a pledge from you before God, and in the past you failed with regard to Joseph? I will not leave this land until my father permits me, or God decides for me; for He is the Best of Deciders.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then, when they had despaired of Joseph they went to a corner and counselled together. The eldest of them said: "Do you not know that your father has taken a solemn promise from you in the name of Allah, and you failed in your duty towards Joseph? So I will not depart from this land until my father permits me, or Allah pronounces His judgement in my favour. He is the best of those who judge."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, when they despaired of him, they consulted in private. The eldest among them said: "Know you not that your father did take an oath from you in Allah's Name, and before this you did fail in your duty with Yusuf Therefore I will not leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah decides my case and He is the Best of the judges.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So, When they despaired of (moving) him, they conferred together apart. The eldest of them said: Know ye not how your father took an undertaking from you in Allah's name and how ye failed in the case of Joseph aforetime? Therefore I shall not go forth from the land until my father giveth leave or Allah judgeth for me. He is the Best of Judges.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: When they had despaired of [moving] him, they withdrew to confer privately. The eldest of them said, ‘Don’t you know that your father has taken a [solemn] pledge from you by Allah, and earlier you have neglected your duty in regard to Joseph? So I will never leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah passes a judgement for me, and He is the best of judges.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: When they despaired of him, they went in private to confer together. The eldest said: 'Do you not know that your father took a pledge from you in the Name of Allah, and that you failed before regarding Joseph? I shall never stir from this land until my father gives me leave or Allah makes known to me His judgement: He is the Best of judges.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when they had despaired of him, they secluded themselves in private consultation. The eldest of them said, "Do you not know that your father has taken upon you an oath by Allah and [that] before you failed in [your duty to] Joseph? So I will never leave [this] land until my father permits me or Allah decides for me, and He is the best of judges.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When they lost all hope (of convincing the Prince), they moved into a corner whispering to each other. The eldest among them said, "Do you not remember that you had solemnly promised our father to return Benjamin to him and that before this you had broken your promise concerning Joseph? I shall never leave this land until my father gives me permission or God decides for me; He is the best Judge.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then when they despaired of him, they retired, conferring privately together. The eldest of them said: Do you not know that your father took from you a covenant in Allah's name, and how you fell short of your duty with respect to Yusuf before? Therefore I will by no means depart from this land until my father permits me or Allah decides for me, and He is the best of the judges:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When they had lost all hope of [persuading] him, they withdrew, conferring among themselves. The eldest said, "Do you not know that your father took from you a pledge in God's name. You have already failed with regard to Joseph, so I shall never leave the land until my father permits me to, or God decides [things] for me. He is the best judge!

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Now when they saw no hope of his (yielding), they held a conference in private. The leader among them said: "Know ye not that your father did take an oath from you in Allah's name, and how, before this, ye did fail in your duty with Joseph? Therefore will I not leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah commands me; and He is the best to command.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So, go to your father and tell him: 'O father, your son has committed a theft. We bear witness to only what we know; we could not prevent the unknown.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Return to your father and then submit, ‘O our father! Indeed your son has stolen; we were witness only to what we know and we were not guardians of the unseen.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Return you all to your father, and say, "Father, thy son stole; we do not testify except that we know; we were no guardians of the Unseen.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Return unto your father, and say: our father! verily thy son hath stolen, and we testify not save according to that which we know; and of the unseen we could not be guards.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Return to your father and say, 'O our father! Verily, your son (Benjamin) has stolen, and we testify not except according to what we know, and we could not know the unseen!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Go back to your father, and say, ‘Our father, your son has stolen. We testify only to what we know, and we could not have prevented the unforeseen.’”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So go back to your father and tell him: "Father! Your son has certainly been guilty of stealing. We did not see him stealing but testify according to what we know, and obviously we had no power to keep watch over that what is altogether hidden from us.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Return to your father and say, `O our father! Verily, your son has stolen, and we testify not except according to what we know, and we could not know the Unseen!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Return unto your father and say: O our father! Lo! thy son hath stolen. We testify only to that which we know; we are not guardians of the Unseen.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Return, all of you to your father and say to him: 'Father, your son has committed a theft. We testify only to what we know. How could we guard against the unforeseen?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Return to your father and say, "O our father, indeed your son has stolen, and we did not testify except to what we knew. And we were not witnesses of the unseen,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "Go to our father and tell him, 'Father, your son committed theft. We say only what we have seen and we have no control over the unseen.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Go back to your father and say: O our father! surely your son committed theft, and we do not bear witness except to what we have known, and we could not keep watch over the unseen:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Return to your father and say, 'Father, your son has committed a theft. We testify only to what we know. How could we guard against the unforeseen?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Turn ye back to your father, and say, 'O our father! behold! thy son committed theft! we bear witness only to what we know, and we could not well guard against the unseen!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Enquire from the people of that city, or ask the men of the caravan with whom we have come. We are verily speaking the truth.'"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And ask the township in which we were, and the caravan in which we came; and indeed we are truthful.’”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Enquire of the city wherein we were, and the caravan in which we approached; surely we are truthful men".'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And inquire those of the city where we have been and of the caravan with whom we have travelled hither; and verily we are truth- tellers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And ask (the people of) the town where we have been, and the caravan in which we returned, and indeed we are telling the truth."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Ask the town where we were, and the caravan in which we came. We are being truthful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: You may inquire of the dwellers of the city where we were, and of the people of the caravan with whom we travelled. We are altogether truthful in what we say."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And ask (the people of) the town where we have been, and the caravan in which we returned; and indeed we are telling the truth."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Ask the township where we were, and the caravan with which we travelled hither. Lo! we speak the truth.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Ask the village where we were and the caravan in which we traveled, we speak the truth'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And ask the city in which we were and the caravan in which we came - and indeed, we are truthful,"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: You can ask the people of the town where we were and the caravan we met there. We are certainly telling the truth."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And inquire in the town in which we were and the caravan with which we proceeded, and most surely we are truthful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Inquire of [the people of] the city where we lodged, and of the caravan with which we travelled. We are telling you the truth.'"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'Ask at the town where we have been and the caravan in which we returned, and (you will find) we are indeed telling the truth.'"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "No," said (the father). "You have made up the story; but patience is best; God may bring them back to me. He is all-knowing and all-wise."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Said Yaqub, “Your souls have fabricated an excuse for you; therefore patience is excellent; it is likely that Allah will bring all* of them to me; undoubtedly only He is the All Knowing, the Wise.” (* All three including Yusuf.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'No!' he said 'But your spirits tempted you to do somewhat. But come, sweet patience! Haply God will bring them all to me; He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: nay! your selves have embellished for you an affair; so seemly patience! Belike Allah may bring them all unto me; verily He! only He Is the Knowing, the Wise.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He [Ya'qub (Jacob)] said: "Nay, but your ownselves have beguiled you into something. So patience is most fitting (for me). May be Allah will bring them (back) all to me. Truly He! only He is All-Knowing, All-Wise."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “Rather, your souls have contrived something for you. Patience is a virtue. Perhaps God will bring them all back to me. He is the Knowing, the Wise.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The father heard the narration and said: "(All that is untrue). But your souls have made it easy for you to engage in a heinous act. So, I will be graciously patient even at this. Allah may well bring them all back to me. He is All-Knowing, All-Wise."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He [Ya`qub] said: "Nay, but your own selves have beguiled you into something. So patience is most fitting (for me). May be Allah will bring them (back) all to me. Truly, He! Only He is All-Knowing, All-Wise."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (And when they came unto their father and had spoken thus to him) he said: Nay, but your minds have beguiled you into something. (My course is) comely patience! It may be that Allah will bring them all unto me. Lo! He, only He, is the Knower, the Wise.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘No, your souls have made a matter seem decorous to you. Yet patience is graceful. Maybe Allah will bring them all [back] to me. Indeed He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'No' he (Jacob) said, 'your souls have tempted you to do something. But come sweet patience. Allah may bring them all to me. He alone is the Knowing, the Wise'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Jacob] said, "Rather, your souls have enticed you to something, so patience is most fitting. Perhaps Allah will bring them to me all together. Indeed it is He who is the Knowing, the Wise."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (When he heard this), Jacob said, "Your souls have tempted you to make up the whole story. Let us be patient for perhaps God will bring them all back to me. God is certainly All-knowing and All-wise".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He (Yaqoub) said: Nay, your souls have made a matter light for you, so patience is good; maybe Allah will bring them all together to me; surely He is the Knowing, the Wise.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Jacob said, "No, but you have yourselves contrived a story. But it is best to be patient. God may well bring them all back to me [in the end]. For He is indeed full of knowledge and wisdom."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Jacob said: "Nay, but ye have yourselves contrived a story (good enough) for you. So patience is most fitting (for me). Maybe Allah will bring them (back) all to me (in the end). For He is indeed full of knowledge and wisdom."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He turned away from them and cried: "Alas for Joseph!" And his eyes turned white with grief which he bore in silence.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And he turned away from them and said, “Alas – the separation from Yusuf!” and his eyes turned white with sorrow, he therefore kept suppressing his anger.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And he turned away from them, and said, 'Ah, woe is me for Joseph!' And his eyes turned white because of the sorrow that he choked within him.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he turned away from them, and said: O my grief for Yusuf! and his eyes whitened with grief, while he was filled with suppressed sorrow.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And he turned away from them and said: "Alas, my grief for Yusuf (Joseph)!" And he lost his sight because of the sorrow that he was suppressing.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then he turned away from them, and said, “O my bitterness for Joseph.” And his eyes turned white from sorrow, and he became depressed.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then he turned his back to them, and said: "O my grief for Joseph!" His eyes whitened with grief and he was choked up with sorrow trying to suppress his grief.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And he turned away from them and said: "Alas, my grief for Yusuf!" And he lost his sight because of the sorrow that he was suppressing.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he turned away from them and said: Alas, my grief for Joseph! And his eyes were whitened with the sorrow that he was suppressing.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He turned away from them and said, ‘Alas for Joseph!’ His eyes had turned white with grief, and he choked with suppressed agony.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And he turned away and said: 'Alas for Joseph' His eyes turned white with the grief he repressed within him.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he turned away from them and said, "Oh, my sorrow over Joseph," and his eyes became white from grief, for he was [of that] a suppressor.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Jacob) turned away from them saying, "Alas, Joseph is lost!" He wept continuously in his grief until, in suppressing his anger, his eyes turned white.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he turned away from them, and said: O my sorrow for Yusuf! and his eyes became white on account of the grief, and he was a repressor (of grief).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And he turned away from them, crying, "Alas for Joseph!" His eyes went white with grief, and he was filled with sorrow.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And he turned away from them, and said: "How great is my grief for Joseph!" And his eyes became white with sorrow, and he fell into silent melancholy.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "By God," said they, "you will never stop thinking of Joseph till you are consumed or perish."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “By Allah, you will keep remembering Yusuf till your health fails you or you lose your life.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'By God,' they said, 'thou wilt never cease mentioning Joseph till thou art consumed, or among the perishing.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: by Allah! thou wilt not cease remembering Yusuf until thou art wizened or thou be of the dead.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! You will never cease remembering Yusuf (Joseph) until you become weak with old age, or until you be of the dead."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “By God, you will not stop remembering Joseph, until you have ruined your health, or you have passed away.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The sons said: "By Allah! You will continue to remember Joseph until you will either consume yourself with grief, or will die."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! You will never cease remembering Yusuf until you become weak with old age, or until you be of the dead."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: By Allah, thou wilt never cease remembering Joseph till thy health is ruined or thou art of those who perish!

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘By Allah! You will go on remembering Joseph until you wreck your health or perish.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'By Allah, will you not cease to mention Joseph until you are consumed, or are among the perishing'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "By Allah, you will not cease remembering Joseph until you become fatally ill or become of those who perish."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "You are always remembering Joseph. By God, it will either make you sick or you will die".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: By Allah! you will not cease to remember Yusuf until you are a prey to constant disease or (until) you are of those who perish.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "By God, will you keep on remembering Joseph until your health is ruined, and you die?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! (never) wilt thou cease to remember Joseph until thou reach the last extremity of illness, or until thou die!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He replied: "I cry my plaint and grief to God, and know from God what you do not know.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He said, “I complain of my worry and grief only to Allah, and I know the great traits of Allah which you do not know.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'I make complaint of my anguish and my sorrow unto God; I know from God that you know not.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: I only bewail my cogitation and grief unto Allah, and I know from Allah that which ye know not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "I only complain of my grief and sorrow to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you know not.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “I only complain of my grief and sorrow to God, and I know from God what you do not know.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He said: "I will address my sorrow and grief only to Allah, and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "I only complain of my grief and sorrow to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you know not."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: I expose my distress and anguish only unto Allah, and I know from Allah that which ye know not.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He replied: 'I complain to Allah of my anguish and sadness. I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "I only complain of my suffering and my grief to Allah, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He replied, "I only complain of my sorrow and grief to God. I know about God what you do not know.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: I only complain of my grief and sorrow to Allah, and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said "I only complain of my anguish and my sorrow to God. God has made known to me things that you do not know.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "I only complain of my distraction and anguish to Allah, and I know from Allah that which ye know not...

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O sons, go in search of Joseph and his brother, and do not despair of the mercy of God. Only they despair of God's mercy who do not believe."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O my sons, go and search for Yusuf and his brother, and do not lose hope in the mercy of Allah; indeed none lose hope in the mercy of Allah except the disbelieving people.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Depart, my sons, and search out tidings of Joseph and his brother. Do not despair of God's comfort; of God's comfort no man despairs, excepting the people of the unbelievers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O My sons! go and ascertain about Yusuf and his brother, and despair not of the comfort of Allah; verily none despair of the comfort of Allah except a people disbelieving.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O my sons! Go you and enquire about Yusuf (Joseph) and his brother, and never give up hope of Allah's Mercy. Certainly no one despairs of Allah's Mercy, except the people who disbelieve."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O my sons, go and inquire about Joseph and his brother, and do not despair of God's comfort. None despairs of God's comfort except the disbelieving people.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My sons! Go and try to find out about Joseph and his brother and do not despair of Allah's mercy. Verily only the unbelievers despair of Allah's mercy."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O my sons! Go you and inquire about Yusuf and his brother, and never give up hope of Allah's mercy. Certainly no one despairs of Allah's mercy, except the people who disbelieve."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Go, O my sons, and ascertain concerning Joseph and his brother, and despair not of the Spirit of Allah. Lo! none despaireth of the Spirit of Allah save disbelieving folk.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: ‘Go, my sons, and look for Joseph and his brother, and do not despair of Allah’s mercy. Indeed no one despairs of Allah’s mercy except the faithless lot.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Go and seek news of Joseph and his brother. Do not despair of the Comfort of Allah, none but unbelievers despair of the Comfort of Allah'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: O my sons, go and find out about Joseph and his brother and despair not of relief from Allah. Indeed, no one despairs of relief from Allah except the disbelieving people."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: My sons, go and search for Joseph and his brother and do not despair of receiving comfort from God; only the unbelievers despair of receiving comfort from Him."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O my sons! Go and inquire respecting Yusuf and his brother, and despair not of Allah's mercy; surely none despairs of Allah's mercy except the unbelieving people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Go, my sons, and seek news of Joseph and his brother. Do not despair of God's mercy; none but those who deny the truth despair of God's mercy."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my sons! go ye and enquire about Joseph and his brother, and never give up hope of Allah's Soothing Mercy: truly no one despairs of Allah's Soothing Mercy, except those who have no faith."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When they returned to him, they said (to Joseph): "O Minister, calamity has befallen us and our people. We have brought but a meagre sum, but give us full measure as alms bestowed. God surely rewards those who give alms."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Then when they reached in the company of Yusuf they said, “O governor! Calamity has struck us and our household, and we have brought goods of little value, so give us the full measure and be generous to us; undoubtedly Allah rewards the generous.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So, when they entered unto him, they said, 'O mighty prince, affliction has visited us and our people. We come with merchandise of scant worth. Fill up to us the measure, and be charitable to us; surely God recompenses the charitable.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And when they entered unto him, they said: O Aziz! distress hath touched us and our household, and we have brought poor goods, wherefore give us full measure, and be charitable unto us; verily Allah recompenseth the charitable.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then, when they entered unto him [Yusuf (Joseph)], they said: "O ruler of the land! A hard time has hit us and our family, and we have brought but poor capital, so pay us full measure and be charitable to us. Truly, Allah does reward the charitable."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, when they entered into his presence, they said, “Mighty governor, adversity has befallen us, and our family. We have brought scant merchandise. But give us full measure, and be charitable towards us—God rewards the charitable.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: On going to Egypt they presented themselves to Joseph and said to him: "O chief! We and our family are struck with distress and have brought only a paltry sum. So give us corn in full measure, and give it to us in charity. Allah rewards those who are charitable."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then, when they entered unto him, they said: "O `Aziz! A hard time has hit us and our family, and we have brought but poor capital, so pay us full measure and be charitable to us. Truly, Allah does reward the charitable."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when they came (again) before him (Joseph) they said: O ruler! Misfortune hath touched us and our folk, and we bring but poor merchandise, so fill for us the measure and be charitable unto us. Lo! Allah will requite the charitable,

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then, when they entered into his presence, they said, ‘O emir! Distress has befallen us and our family, and we have brought [just] a meagre sum. Yet grant us the full measure, and be charitable to us! Indeed Allah rewards the charitable.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when they presented themselves before him they said: 'O mighty prince, we and our people are afflicted with distress. We have brought but little merchandise. Fill up the measure, and be charitable to us; Allah rewards the charitable'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when they entered upon Joseph, they said, "O 'Azeez, adversity has touched us and our family, and we have come with goods poor in quality, but give us full measure and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards the charitable."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When they entered Joseph's court, they said, "Noble Prince, hardship has struck us and our people. We have come with a little money, so give us a measure of grain and be charitable to us. God will give the reward to those who give charity".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when they came in to him, they said: O chief! distress has afflicted us and our family and we have brought scanty money, so give us full measure and be charitable to us; surely Allah rewards the charitable.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When his brothers presented themselves before Joseph, they pleaded, "Exalted one, distress has afflicted us and our family and we have brought only a paltry sum; but give us full measure. Be charitable to us. Truly, God rewards the charitable."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then, when they came (back) into (Joseph's) presence they said: "O exalted one! distress has seized us and our family: we have (now) brought but scanty capital: so pay us full measure, (we pray thee), and treat it as charity to us: for Allah doth reward the charitable."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: , He said: "Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother in your ignorance?"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He said, “Are you aware of what you did to Yusuf and his brother when you were unwise?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'Are you aware of what you did with Joseph and his brother, when you were ignorant?'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: remember ye that which ye did Unto Yusuf and his brother while ye were ignorant?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "Do you know what you did with Yusuf (Joseph) and his brother, when you were ignorant?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “Do you realize what you did with Joseph and his brother, in your ignorance?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When Joseph heard this (he could not hold himself and said): "Do you remember what you did to Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "Do you know what you did with Yusuf and his brother, when you were ignorant"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Know ye what ye did unto Joseph and his brother in your ignorance?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘Have you realized what you did to Joseph and his brother, when you were senseless?’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Do you know' he replied, 'what you did to Joseph and his brother in your ignorance'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "Do you know what you did with Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Joseph asked them, "Do you know what you did to Joseph and his brother in your ignorance?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: Do you know how you treated Yusuf and his brother when you were ignorant?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "Are you aware of what you did to Joseph and his brother in ignorance?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Know ye how ye dealt with Joseph and his brother, not knowing (what ye were doing)?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "Surely you are not Joseph!" "I am Joseph," he said, "and this is my brother. God has been gracious to us; for God does not verily deprive those who fear Him and are patient of the recompense of those who are good."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “Are you, in truth you, really Yusuf?” He said, “I am Yusuf and this is my brother; indeed Allah has bestowed favour upon us; undoubtedly whoever practices piety and patience – so Allah does not waste the wages of the righteous.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'Why, art thou indeed Joseph?' 'I am Joseph,' he said. 'This is my brother. God has indeed been gracious unto us. Whosoever fears God, and is patient -- surely God leaves not to waste the wage of the good-doers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: art thou Yusuf indeed! He said: I am Yusuf and this is my brother; Allah hath surely been gracious unto us; verily whosoever feareth and endureth, then verily Allah wasteth not the hire of the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "Are you indeed Yusuf (Joseph)?" He said: "I am Yusuf (Joseph), and this is my brother (Benjamin). Allah has indeed been gracious to us. Verily, he who fears Allah with obedience to Him (by abstaining from sins and evil deeds, and by performing righteous good deeds), and is patient, then surely, Allah makes not the reward of the Muhsinun (good-doers - see V. 2:112) to be lost."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “Is that you, Joseph?” He said, “I am Joseph, and this is my brother. God has been gracious to us. He who practices piety and patience—God never fails to reward the righteous.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They exclaimed: "Are you indeed Joseph?" He said: "Yes, I am Joseph and this is my brother. Allah has surely been gracious to us. Indeed whoever fears Allah and remains patient, Allah does not allow the reward of such people to go to waste."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "Are you indeed Yusuf" He said: "I am Yusuf, and this is my brother. Allah has indeed been gracious to us. He who has Taqwa, and is patient, then surely, Allah makes not the reward of the gooddoers to be lost."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: Is it indeed thou who art Joseph? He said: I am Joseph and this is my brother. Allah hath shown us favour. Lo! he who wardeth off (evil) and endureth (findeth favour); for lo! Allah loseth not the wages of the kindly.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘Are you really Joseph?!’ He said, ‘I am Joseph, and this is my brother. Certainly Allah has shown us favour. Indeed if one is Godwary and patient Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'Are you Joseph' 'I am Joseph' he answered, 'and this is my brother. Allah has been gracious to us. Those who keep from evil and are patient, indeed, Allah does not let the wage of the good doers go to waste'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "Are you indeed Joseph?" He said "I am Joseph, and this is my brother. Allah has certainly favored us. Indeed, he who fears Allah and is patient, then indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Then they inquired, "Are you Joseph?" He said, "Yes, I am Joseph and this is my brother. God has indeed been gracious to us. One who exercises patience and observes piety should know that God does not ignore the reward of the righteous ones."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: Are you indeed Yusuf? He said: I am Yusuf and this is my brother; Allah has indeed been gracious to us; surely he who guards (against evil) and is patient (is rewarded) for surely Allah does not waste the reward of those who do good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They exclaimed, "Are you indeed Joseph?" He replied, "I am Joseph and this is my brother. God has indeed been gracious to us. The truth is that God does not waste the reward of those who do good, who are righteous and steadfast."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "Art thou indeed Joseph?" He said, "I am Joseph, and this is my brother: Allah has indeed been gracious to us (all): behold, he that is righteous and patient,- never will Allah suffer the reward to be lost, of those who do right."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "By God, God has favoured you above us, for we have indeed been sinners."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “By Allah, undoubtedly Allah has given you superiority over us, and we were indeed guilty.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'By God,' they said, 'God has indeed preferred thee above us, and certainly we have been sinful.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: by Allah! assuredly Allah hath chosen thee above us, and we have been sinners indeed.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! Indeed Allah has preferred you above us, and we certainly have been sinners."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “By God, God has preferred you over us. We were definitely in the wrong.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "We swear by Allah! Indeed Allah has chosen you in preference to us and we were truly guilty."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! Indeed Allah has preferred you above us, and we certainly have been sinners."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: By Allah, verily Allah hath preferred thee above us, and we were indeed sinful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'By Allah' they said, 'Allah has preferred you above us all. We have indeed been sinful'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "By Allah, certainly has Allah preferred you over us, and indeed, we have been sinners."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "We swear by God that He has given preference to you over us and we have sinned".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: By Allah! now has Allah certainly chosen you over us, and we were certainly sinners.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: [The brothers] said, "By God! Most certainly God has raised you high above us, and we have indeed been guilty!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "By Allah! indeed has Allah preferred thee above us, and we certainly have been guilty of sin!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "There is no blame on you today," he said, "May God forgive you. He is the most merciful of all.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'No reproach this day shall be on you; God will forgive you; He is the most merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said; no reproach upon you today. May Allah forgive you; and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "No reproach on you this day, may Allah forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of those who show mercy!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “There is no blame upon you today. God will forgive you. He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He replied: "No blame lies with you today. May Allah forgive you. He is the Most Merciful of all those that are merciful.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "No reproach on you this day; may Allah forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of those who show mercy!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Have no fear this day! May Allah forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of those who show mercy.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘There shall be no reproach on you today. Allah will forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He replied: 'Let no reproach be on you this day. Allah will forgive you He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "No blame will there be upon you today. Allah will forgive you; and He is the most merciful of the merciful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Joseph) said, "No one will blame you on this day. God will forgive you; He is more Merciful than others.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: (There shall be) no reproof against you this day; Allah may forgive you, and He is the most Merciful of the merciful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "No blame [shall fall] on you this day; may God forgive you! And He is the Most Merciful of those who show mercy.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "This day let no reproach be (cast) on you: Allah will forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of those who show mercy!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Take my shirt and put it on my father's face; his eyesight will be restored; and bring your entire family to me."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Take along this shirt of mine and lay it on my father’s face, his vision will be restored; and bring your entire household to me.” (Prophet Yusuf knew that this miracle would occur.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Go, take this shirt, and do you cast it on my father's face, and he shall recover his sight; then bring me your family all together.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Go with this shirt of mine and cast it upon my father's face; he shall become clear-sighted; and bring unto me all your household.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Go with this shirt of mine, and cast it over the face of my father, he will become clear-sighted, and bring to me all your family."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Take this shirt of mine, and lay it over my father’s face, and he will recover his sight. And bring your whole family to me.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Take this shirt of mine and throw it over my father's face. He will regain his sight. And bring to me all your family."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Go with this shirt of mine, and cast it over the face of my father, his vision will return, and bring to me all your family."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Go with this shirt of mine and lay it on my father's face, he will become (again) a seer; and come to me with all your folk.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Take this shirt of mine, and cast it upon my father’s face; he will regain his sight, and bring me all your folks.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Go, take this shirt of mine and cast it over my father's face, he will recover his sight. Then return to me with all your family'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Take this, my shirt, and cast it over the face of my father; he will become seeing. And bring me your family, all together."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Take my shirt and place it unto my father's face. This will restore his eye-sight. Then bring the whole family to me."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Take this my shirt and cast it on my father's face, he will (again) be able to see, and come to me with all your families.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Go with this shirt of mine and cast it upon my father's face. He will recover his sight; thereupon come [back] to me with all your family."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Go with this my shirt, and cast it over the face of my father: he will come to see (clearly). Then come ye (here) to me together with all your family."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The caravan departed (from Egypt) and Jacob said (at home): "Say not that I am in my dotage, but I get the smell of Joseph."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: When the caravan left Egypt, their father said*, “Indeed I sense the fragrance of Yusuf, if you do not call me senile.” (* Prophet Yaqub said this in Palestine, to other members of his family. He could discern the fragrance of Prophet Yusuf’s shirt from far away.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So, when the caravan set forth, their father said, 'Surely I perceive Joseph's scent, unless you think me doting.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And when the caravan had departed, their father said: surely feel the smell of Yusuf, if ye think not that I am doting.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And when the caravan departed, their father said: "I do indeed feel the smell of Yusuf (Joseph), if only you think me not a dotard (a person who has weakness of mind because of old age)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: As the caravan set out, their father said, “I sense the presence of Joseph, though you may think I am senile.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And as the caravan set out (from Egypt), their father said (in Canaan): "Indeed I smell the fragrance of Joseph. I say so although you may think that I am doting."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And when the caravan departed, their father said: "I do indeed sense the smell of Yusuf, if only you think me not senile."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When the caravan departed their father had said: Truly I am conscious of the breath of Joseph, though ye call me dotard.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: As the caravan set off, their father said, ‘I sense the scent of Joseph, if you will not consider me a dotard.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: As the caravan departed, their father said: 'I smell the scent of Joseph, unless you think I am foolish'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And when the caravan departed [from Egypt], their father said, "Indeed, I find the smell of Joseph [and would say that he was alive] if you did not think me weakened in mind."
